reasoning: The question asks whether any MEP will announce their resignation between June 9 and July 1, 2025. Although a major Huawei lobbying scandal currently names several MEPs (with Belgian prosecutors requesting the lifting of immunity for four or five members), precedent from similar recent scandals (e.g., Qatargate) strongly suggests these MEPs will not resign. In Qatargate, implicated MEPs like Eva Kaili and Marc Tarabella retained their seats even while spending months in pre-trial detention. Furthermore, news reports explicitly state that the European Parliament will not process these immunity waivers in substance until September 2025, completely removing any immediate timeline pressure. All named MEPs have maintained their innocence and stated their intention to cooperate. Therefore, any resignation in this brief 3-week window would largely rely on the background base rate (due to health, personal reasons, or national political appointments). Given the large 720-member body, a 10-15% chance reflects this background risk, but I am confident the current Huawei scandal will not force an imminent resignation.
